figures.caption: Figure 4: Evolution of 1000 perturbed trajectories starting from x0 = [0.4, 0.4]⊤. Red, green, and blue denote the boundaries of the sets U , T , and S, respectively.
Figure 1: Comparison of bounds obtained using the proposed approach, element-wise absolute-value bounds, and Grönwall-based estimates.
Figure 3: Evolution of 1000 perturbed trajectories in the temperature regulation case. Green and blue correspond to the specifications for TS1 and TS2 , respectively.

Figure 5: (a) Carla simulation environment with the fire truck V1 (red) and autonomous vehicle V2 (blue) at an intersection. Video Link (b) Nominal behavior with no disturbance. (c) Collision avoidance when the disturbance is bounded by the computed maximum disturbance. (d) Collision when the disturbance exceeds the computed maximum disturbance.
Figure 2: Evolution of 1000 perturbed trajectories in the DC Motor case. Green lines mark the safe and target set boundaries, showing all trajectories satisfy ψ1 and ψ2.
